page: Microsoft Azure D32pds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an ARM-based Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz, with 128.0 GB of RAM providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. The system includes 1890 GB of local NVMe storage and is premium storage capable, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance and exceptional memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier status in uncached memory bandwidth read and write tests, as well as PassMark memory and database operations. Multi-core performance is strong, as evidenced by stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores, alongside high throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving. However, single-core performance and complex tasks like large-scale LLM inference prompt processing show average to poor performance, reflecting the architectural tradeoffs of the ARM Neoverse-N2 design.

The D32pds_v6 offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. The combination of 128 GB of RAM, a 1890 GB local disk, and top-tier database operation benchmarks makes this server highly suitable for relational databases, Redis caching layers, static web serving, and software compilation pipelines. It is less suited for workloads requiring specialized x86 vector instructions or heavy LLM inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM and 1890 GB of local storage, this general-purpose instance delivers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier memory bandwidth and database operations, alongside strong multi-core processing capabilities. However, single-core performance remains average, and the lack of x86-specific vector instructions limits its suitability for certain high-performance computing tasks. The D32pds_v6 is qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded, memory-intensive workloads, making it highly suitable for database hosting, Redis caching, web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 32 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, offering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32pds_v6 is a 32-vCPU ARM virtual machine with 128 GB RAM and 1890 GB local storage, optimized for databases and multi-threaded tasks.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed local storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for database and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-core ARM Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM and 1890 GB of local storage
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation performance
Features dedicated physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio
Best suited for database hosting, Redis caching, and web serving
